bbc.title: Mexico sends thousands of soldiers to stop violence after death of drug lord
bbc.description: A wave of violence has erupted across Mexico since a powerful drug cartel boss died following his capture by special forces.

bbc.title: More than 5,000 flights cancelled as US east coast digs out of record snow
bbc.description: A major snowstorm walloped the US north-east, causing disruptions for millions and thousands of flight cancellations.

bbc.title: US partially evacuates Beirut embassy amid rising Iran tensions
bbc.description: The State Department has ordered non-essential staff to leave the embassy in Beirut after a security review.

bbc.title: Four years into its full-scale war in Ukraine, Russia is feeling the effects
bbc.description: Steve Rosenberg reports on the economic consequences of Russia's war, and how people are coping.

bbc.title: Ukraine negotiator tells BBC how it feels to sit across table from Russia
bbc.description: Sergiy Kyslytsya is among those trying to negotiate an end to the conflict, which is entering its fifth year.

bbc.title: French minister moves to block US envoy Kushner from government access
bbc.description: The minister says Charles Kushner, father of Trump's son-in-law Jared, had failed to explain US comments about violence in France.

bbc.title: Rob Jetten becomes Netherlands' youngest ever PM
bbc.description: The 38-year-old is sworn in as premier after clinching a narrow victory in October's election.

bbc.title: Trump threatens countries that 'play games' with existing trade deals
bbc.description: The threat comes after the Supreme Court ruled on Friday that Trump had exceeded his authority in enacting a sweeping global programme of tariffs.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
wsj.description: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: Chinese Stocks Gain After Holiday on Tariff Relief, AI Buzz
bloomberg.description: China’s onshore shares rose as traders returned from a nine-day Lunar New Year break, with optimism about lower US tariffs and homegrown technologies offsetting mixed signals on holiday spending.

bloomberg.title: Asian Stocks Avoid AI Angst in US, China Gains: Markets Wrap
bloomberg.description: Asian shares mostly bucked a US selloff sparked by anxiety over the impact of artificial intelligence on company profits that rattled Wall Street. Gold and silver fell.

bloomberg.title: Hong Kong Is Near End of Budget Deficit Era But Caution Prevails
bloomberg.description: For a city on the cusp of ending its longest run of budget deficits in two decades, Hong Kong isn’t brimming with confidence just yet.

bloomberg.description: Oil steadied as US President Donald Trump said his preference was for a nuclear deal with Iran before talks this week, though he warned of consequences if an agreement wasn’t reached.

aljazeera.description: Diplomacy continues despite the significant United States military build-up.

aljazeera.description: The White House is set to impose a 15 percent tariff through Section 122 after the US Supreme Court ruled against Trump.

aljazeera.description: At the BAFTA Film Awards, a guest with Tourette syndrome involuntarily shouted a racial slur during the BBC’s broadcast.

washpost.title: CIA intelligence helped Mexican forces track down slain cartel boss
washpost.description: The killing of El Mencho triggered violence across Mexico. In cities including Puerto Vallarta and Cancún, the U.S. warned citizens to shelter in place.

washpost.title: Mexico’s most powerful cartel leader is killed by security forces
washpost.description: The Mexican military’s killing of Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, a.k.a. “El Mencho,” set off violence in areas controlled by the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

washpost.title: Jack Hughes loses some teeth, picks up gold in epic U.S. win over Canada

washpost.title: Trump’s talk of sending a hospital ship to Greenland puzzles leaders
washpost.description: Trump says he’s sending a ship to care for Greenland’s sick. But the territory doesn’t want the help and the U.S. appears to have no hospital ships available to send.
